physics_paths.path: Reaction Wheel -> Motor Mount -> CubeSat Structure
physics_paths.description: Torque generated by reaction wheels is transferred through motor mount to the CubeSat structure.
Rotation of the motor shaft drives the reaction wheel, changing the CubeSat's angular momentum.
Heat generated by the motor is dissipated through a heat sink and transferred to the CubeSat structure.

internal_feature_targets.description: Mount for the motor to the reaction wheel module.
Reaction wheel that changes the CubeSat's angular momentum.
Heat sink that dissipates heat from the motor.
Connector for power distribution from the CubeSat's power plane.
Connector for control signals from the CubeSat's control board.

integration_opportunities.description: Optimize the motor mount for efficient transfer of torque.
Improve the heat sink design for better thermal management.
Ensure robust electrical connections for power and control signals.

verification_focus.verification: measure torque output of the reaction wheel
test continuity and insulation of power and control connectors

generated_parts.label: EPS Solar Charging Module
generated_parts.spec: EPS solar charging subsystem: solar_panel_face inputs, removable battery_tray, eps_board_slot, power_connector keying, service cover, mass budget <480g

requirements: EPS solar charging subsystem: solar_panel_face inputs, removable battery_tray, eps_board_slot, power_connector keying, service cover, mass budget <480g>
Discipline: power - role: Solar panel dummies, battery tray, charge/distribution board
Suggested material: Al 7075 + CFRP + FR4, process: CNC + PCB
Required evidence_features: solar_panel_face, battery_tray, eps_board_slot, power_connector
Replaceable_module_path: Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Cable_harness_visibility: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access_not_just_text: Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover

Harness_strain_relief_and_bend_radius: Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connector_accessibility_and_keying: Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Power_signal_separation: Motor power lines and sensor/encoder signal lines run in separate channels or are spaced/crossed at right angles, with the separation visible in routing geometry
Return_path_and_grounding_intent: Each electronics module states its grounding scheme (star point, chassis bond, isolated) and signal returns route alongside their signals
Board_mounting_and_clearance: PCBs mount on standoffs with keep-out clearance to walls and moving parts, connector edges face access openings, and mounting holes match standard board patterns
